name: IRC_ROUTING_STEPS_B
description: This table stores all the routing steps involved in a process

columns.Name: ROUTING_STEP_ID
columns.Comments: Primary key of the table. System generated.

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: TYPE_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores the type of the routing as a a lookup code.

columns.Name: PROCESS_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key to IRC_PROCESSES_B table.

columns.Name: SEQUENCE_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Determine the sequence number of the step whitin the immediate parent process

columns.Name: SUB_PROCESS_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign keyto IRC_PROCESSES_B table. Stores the reference to the sub process within a process.

columns.Name: PHASE_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key to IRC_PHASES_B table.

columns.Name: STATE_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key to IRC_STATES_B table.

columns.Name: PUBLIC_STATE_ID
columns.Comments: PUBLIC_STATE_ID

columns.Name: IS_INITIAL_FLAG
columns.Comments: Indicates that this routing step it's the initial one (used for routing of type state)

columns.Name: IS_COMPLETION_FLAG
columns.Comments: Indicates that this routing step marks phase as completed (used for routing of type state)

columns.Name: LAST_ACTIVATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Date when the step has been activated

columns.Name: LAST_DEACTIVATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Date when a step has been deactivated

columns.Name: STEP_STATUS
columns.Comments: Value from FND lookup, will determinate the status of the step (Active / Inactive ... )

columns.Name: STEP_TEMPLATE_ID
columns.Comments: Field used by the instance to know from which step it has been instantiated.

columns.Name: SETTING_ID
columns.Comments: Points to a setting id when needed, this value will be the same for the step instance and for the step template.

columns.Name: CODE
columns.Comments: Identifies a step uniquely among all other steps sharing a same processId

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.
